overview: Lorwyn Eclipsed returns to a world of duality. While the sun sets, the tribal alliances of Lorwyn must adapt to the creeping shadows of Shadowmoor. The set is heavily tribal-focused with a darker twist: -1/-1 counters and Wither represent the encroaching eclipse. To succeed, you must commit to a tribe while navigating the complex combat math of the Wither mechanic.

mechanics.name: Kinship
mechanics.description: At the beginning of your upkeep, you may look at the top card of your library. If it shares a creature type, you gain a bonus.
mechanics.draft_tip: Tribal density is key. Aim for 12+ creatures of your primary type to make Kinship reliable.

mechanics.name: Wither
mechanics.description: This creature deals damage to creatures in the form of -1/-1 counters.
mechanics.draft_tip: Wither is excellent for taking down large threats over multiple turns. It also permanently shuts down 'Persist' creatures.

mechanics.name: Thoughtweft (Shared Abilities)
mechanics.description: Kithkin creatures that grant abilities to all other Kithkin you control.
mechanics.draft_tip: Prioritize creatures that grant keywords like First Strike or Vigilance to the whole team; they scale exponentially.

mechanics.name: Persist
mechanics.description: When this creature dies, if it had no -1/-1 counters on it, return it to the battlefield with a -1/-1 counter.
mechanics.draft_tip: Look for ways to remove -1/-1 counters (like +1/+1 counters or blink effects) to make your creatures effectively immortal.

archetypes.name: Merfolk Tapping
archetypes.description: Use a variety of tricky tapping synergies to cast your spells for cheap and disrupt your opponents.
archetypes.strategy: Control the board by tapping down the opponent's best blockers. Use 'Tapped matters' payoffs to generate mana or card advantage.

archetypes.name: Goblins -1/-1 Counters
archetypes.description: Throw boggarts at every problem! Find ways to put -1/-1 counters on your disposable Goblin forces and reap rewards.
archetypes.strategy: Aggressive sacrifice. Use Goblins with Persist and sacrifice them to deal damage or place counters on enemy threats.

archetypes.name: Kithkin Thoughtweft
archetypes.description: Use the power of shared consciousness to spread abilities among Kithkin. The more Kithkin you play, the greater your thoughtweft grows!
archetypes.strategy: Go-wide aggro. Build a massive board of small Kithkin and use 'Anthem' lords to make them impossible to block profitably.

archetypes.name: Elementals Spells
archetypes.description: Burn bright like a flamekin. Cast big spells to unleash seismic Elemental power!
archetypes.strategy: Midrange/Control. Use efficient removal to survive the early game, then cast high-CMC Elementals or big 'X' spells to close the game.

archetypes.name: Elves Graveyard
archetypes.description: Harness the cycle of nature and use your graveyard as a resource to overwhelm your opponents.
archetypes.strategy: Token generation and recursion. Use Elves that create tokens whenever you cast an Elf spell, and bring back key pieces from the yard.

archetypes.name: Faerie Flash
archetypes.description: The masters of the night. Use flash, flying, and disruption to win without ever tapping out on your own turn.
archetypes.strategy: Pure tempo. Play everything at the end of your opponent's turn. Counter their key spells and chip away with small flyers.

archetypes.name: Giant Stomp
archetypes.description: Raw power. Play the biggest creatures in the set and trample over anyone small enough to stand in the way.
archetypes.strategy: Ramp into Giants. Use cost-reducers to play 6/6s on turn 4. This deck struggles against deathtouch but dominates ground combat.

archetypes.name: Cinder Attrition
archetypes.description: A slow, painful death. Use lifelink and life-drain effects to stay alive while your opponent withers away.
archetypes.strategy: Drain and Gain. Focus on creatures that trigger when you gain life or when the opponent loses life. Grind them out.

archetypes.name: Flamekin Aggro
archetypes.description: High velocity. Use haste creatures and cheap combat tricks to end the game before the sun even sets.
archetypes.strategy: Hyper-aggro. Every creature should have haste or provide a power boost. Don't worry about the late game; there isn't one.

archetypes.name: Treefolk Ramp
archetypes.description: Slow and steady wins the race. Ramp into massive Treefolk that care about toughness and longevity.
archetypes.strategy: Survival. Use high-toughness blockers to stall the early game. Use Kinship to play Treefolk for free from the top of your deck.

general_tips: Wither changes how you block. Even a 1/1 can permanently weaken a 5/5, so don't be afraid to chump-block.
Tribal synergies are 'all-or-nothing'. If you are drafting Elves, avoid picking non-Elf creatures unless they are top-tier bombs.
Nameless Inversion is the most flexible card in the set; it counts as every creature type, triggering all Kinship and tribal effects.
Persist creatures are great for sacrifice decks, but watch out for Wither removal which negates the Persist trigger.
The 'Eclipse' makes the format grindier than Bloomburrow. Prioritize card advantage and ways to break board stalls.
